obligations.text: Bureau to prescribe such rules and issue such guidance as may be necessary to carry out, enforce, and compile data pursuant to section 1071
obligations.plain_language: The CFPB must create rules and guidance to implement section 1071 requirements for small business lending data collection and enforcement.
obligations.action_required: Prescribe rules and issue guidance necessary to carry out, enforce, and compile data under section 1071

obligations.text: Financial institutions must collect and report data on small business lending
obligations.plain_language: Banks and other financial institutions are required to gather and submit information about their small business lending activities.
obligations.action_required: Collect and report data on small business lending activities
obligations.trigger_condition: As specified in Regulation B, subpart B compliance dates
obligations.exemptions: Those not subject to the rule's compliance deadlines per court orders

deadlines.action: Final rule implementing section 1071 by adding subpart B to Regulation B became effective
deadlines.who: all financial institutions subject to Regulation B

deadlines.action: Interim final rule published extending compliance dates for data collection and reporting on small business lending
deadlines.who: financial institutions preparing to comply with section 1071
deadlines.consequence: Extended compliance deadlines for certain market participants per court orders

deadlines.action: Interim final rule published with further amendments to Regulation B, subpart B
deadlines.who: all financial institutions subject to small business lending requirements
deadlines.consequence: Additional amendments to coverage, definitions, data points, and compliance dates became effective

exemptions.who: Plaintiffs and intervenors in litigation challenging the 2023 final rule
exemptions.condition: In three jurisdictions where courts stayed the rule's compliance deadlines
Courts did not stay compliance dates for those not parties to litigation

definitions.definition: Definition subject to amendment in this final rule regarding small business definition
definitions.relevance: Determines which credit transactions and financial institutions are covered by Regulation B, subpart B requirements

definitions.definition: Section 1071 of the Dodd-Frank Wall Street Reform and Consumer Protection Act directing the Bureau to prescribe rules and guidance for small business lending data collection and enforcement
definitions.relevance: The statutory authority and mandate for Regulation B, subpart B

definitions.definition: Regulatory provisions implementing section 1071 requirements for small business lending under the Equal Credit Opportunity Act
definitions.relevance: The regulatory framework governing small business lending data collection, reporting, and compliance obligations

obligations.text: financial institutions collect and report to the Bureau certain data regarding applications for credit for women-owned, minority-owned, and small businesses
obligations.plain_language: Financial institutions must gather and submit specific data about credit applications from small businesses and women/minority-owned businesses to the CFPB.
obligations.action_required: collect and report data regarding applications for credit
obligations.trigger_condition: when applications for credit are received for small businesses, women-owned businesses, or minority-owned businesses
obligations.section_reference: Section 1071 of Dodd-Frank Act, amending ECOA

deadlines.consequence: rule takes effect and institutions must begin compliance preparations
institutions must be in full compliance with all requirements; failure to comply may result in enforcement action

deadlines.action: final rule published in Federal Register confirming 2025 interim final rule findings
deadlines.consequence: establishes final compliance framework with no further substantive changes

definitions.definition: provision of the Dodd-Frank Wall Street Reform and Consumer Protection Act that amended the Equal Credit Opportunity Act to require collection and reporting of data regarding applications for credit for small businesses, women-owned businesses, and minority-owned businesses
definitions.relevance: defines the statutory authority and scope of the regulatory requirements for small business lending data collection

definitions.definition: CFPB regulation implementing the Equal Credit Opportunity Act
definitions.relevance: the regulation being amended to implement Section 1071 requirements

definitions.definition: Equal Credit Opportunity Act
definitions.relevance: the underlying federal statute being amended by Section 1071 to require small business lending data collection

definitions.definition: financial institutions subject to the Section 1071 reporting requirements, including banks, credit unions, community development financial institutions (CDFIs), community development companies, Farm Credit System (FCS) lenders, and online lenders
definitions.relevance: identifies which entities must comply with the data collection and reporting obligations

obligations.text: facilitate enforcement of fair lending laws
obligations.plain_language: Financial institutions must support efforts to enforce fair lending regulations
obligations.action_required: Cooperate with fair lending enforcement activities

obligations.text: enable communities, governmental organizations, entities, and creditors to identify business and community development needs and opportunities of women-owned, minority-owned, and small businesses
obligations.plain_language: Institutions must provide data that helps identify lending needs for women-owned, minority-owned, and small businesses
obligations.who_must_comply: all financial institutions subject to section 1071
obligations.action_required: Collect and report small business lending data as specified in regulations

obligations.text: The Bureau received approximately 410 comments on the 2025 proposed rule
obligations.plain_language: The CFPB accepted public comments on proposed amendments to small business lending data collection rules
obligations.action_required: Submit comments during designated comment periods
obligations.trigger_condition: When CFPB publishes proposed rules in Federal Register
obligations.penalty: Comments submitted after deadline may not be considered
obligations.section_reference: 2025 proposed rule, Federal Register Vol. 91, No. 84

obligations.text: The Bureau believes that it should approach the section 1071 data collection regime as a longer-term project akin to HMDA
obligations.plain_language: The CFPB intends to implement section 1071 data collection gradually, similar to how HMDA was implemented
obligations.who_must_comply: all financial institutions subject to section 1071
obligations.action_required: Prepare for phased implementation of data collection requirements

obligations.text: commence the collection of data with a narrower scope to ensure its quality and to limit, as much as possible, any disturbance of the provision and availability of credit
obligations.plain_language: Data collection will start with limited scope to maintain data quality and avoid disrupting credit availability
obligations.who_must_comply: all financial institutions subject to section 1071
obligations.action_required: Comply with narrower initial data collection scope as defined in final rule
obligations.trigger_condition: Upon final rule issuance and effective date

deadlines.action: Submit comments on 2025 proposed rule to amend 2023 final rule
deadlines.consequence: Comments submitted after this date may not be considered in rulemaking

deadlines.action: CFPB published 2025 proposed rule in Federal Register
deadlines.consequence: Initiated comment period for proposed amendments

definitions.definition: Public Law 111–203, tit. X, sec. 1071, 124 Stat. 1376, 2056 (2010), codified at ECOA sec. 704B, 15 U.S.C. 1691c–2
definitions.relevance: Establishes authority for CFPB to require small business lending data collection to facilitate fair lending enforcement and identify community development needs

definitions.definition: Equal Credit Opportunity Act, 15 U.S.C. 1691 et seq.
definitions.relevance: Federal law prohibiting discrimination in credit transactions; section 704B (section 1071) requires data collection on small business lending

definitions.definition: Categories of businesses for which lending data must be collected and analyzed
definitions.relevance: Identifies the specific business categories that are focus of section 1071 data collection requirements

definitions.definition: Home Mortgage Disclosure Act; referenced as model for phased implementation approach
definitions.relevance: The CFPB intends to implement section 1071 data collection using a similar long-term, phased approach as was used for HMDA

definitions.definition: Final rule implementing section 1071 requirements issued in 2023
definitions.relevance: Baseline regulation being amended by 2025 proposed rule; some provisions remain unchanged while others are modified

definitions.definition: Interim final rule addressing section 1071 requirements published in 2025
definitions.relevance: Addressed complexity and scope issues; subject to further amendment through 2025 proposed rule

definitions.definition: Proposed amendments to 2023 final rule published November 13, 2025
definitions.relevance: Seeks to modify data collection scope and requirements; comment period closed December 15, 2025

obligations.text: the Bureau now believes that an incremental approach will better serve the statutory purposes of section 1071 in the long term. Such an approach will start with core lending products, core providers, and core data points.
obligations.plain_language: The CFPB will implement section 1071 data collection gradually, beginning with essential lending products, major lenders, and key data points.
obligations.action_required: Comply with an incremental approach to data collection starting with core lending products, core providers, and core data points
obligations.trigger_condition: Implementation of section 1071 small business lending data collection

obligations.text: The Bureau has consulted with the appropriate prudential regulators and other Federal agencies regarding consistency with any prudential, market, or systemic objectives
obligations.plain_language: The CFPB must coordinate with prudential regulators and other federal agencies to ensure consistency with their objectives.
obligations.action_required: Consult with appropriate prudential regulators and other Federal agencies regarding prudential, market, or systemic objectives

obligations.text: Federal agencies to remove references and questions discussing gender identity
obligations.plain_language: Federal agencies must eliminate questions and references about gender identity from data collection.
obligations.who_must_comply: all financial institutions subject to federal data collection requirements
obligations.action_required: Remove references and questions discussing gender identity from data collection instruments
obligations.trigger_condition: Pursuant to Executive Order 14168 issued January 20, 2025
obligations.section_reference: Executive Order 14168, Defending Women From Gender Ideology Extremism and Restoring Biological Truth to the Federal Government

obligations.text: directing agencies to use those terms when seeking information about an individual's sex
obligations.plain_language: When collecting information about sex, agencies must use binary male/female categories only.
obligations.who_must_comply: all financial institutions subject to federal data collection requirements
obligations.action_required: Use binary male/female sex categories when collecting information about individuals
obligations.trigger_condition: Pursuant to Executive Order 14168 issued January 20, 2025
obligations.section_reference: Executive Order 14168, Defending Women From Gender Ideology Extremism and Restoring Biological Truth to the Federal Government

prohibitions.text: remove references and questions discussing gender identity
prohibitions.plain_language: Questions and references about gender identity are prohibited in federal data collection.
prohibitions.prohibited_action: Including references to or questions about gender identity in data collection
prohibitions.scope: All Federal agencies and financial institutions subject to federal data collection requirements

definitions.definition: Essential or primary lending products that will be the initial focus of section 1071 data collection
definitions.relevance: Determines which loan types must be reported first under the incremental implementation approach

definitions.definition: Primary financial institutions including banks, credit unions, CDFIs, FCS lenders, and online lenders that will be initial focus of data collection
definitions.relevance: Identifies which lenders must comply with initial section 1071 reporting requirements

definitions.definition: Essential data elements that will be collected in the initial phase of section 1071 implementation
definitions.relevance: Specifies which data fields lenders must collect and report in the first iteration of the rule

definitions.definition: Statutory provision requiring collection of small business lending data under the Equal Credit Opportunity Act
definitions.relevance: The primary statutory authority governing small business lending data collection requirements

definitions.definition: A phased implementation strategy starting with modest requirements and core products, with potential expansion over time
definitions.relevance: Defines the implementation methodology for section 1071 data collection, allowing for future amendments as experience is gained

obligations.text: financial institutions to collect and report to the Bureau certain data on applications for small business lending
obligations.plain_language: Financial institutions must gather and submit specific information about small business loan applications to the CFPB.
obligations.action_required: collect and report data on small business lending applications
obligations.trigger_condition: when financial institutions receive applications for small business lending
obligations.exemptions: smaller lenders may face difficulties with compliance

exemptions.condition: smaller lenders would face difficulties complying with data collection requirements

definitions.definition: applications for credit by small business entities, subject to collection and reporting requirements under section 1071
definitions.relevance: defines the scope of transactions and data that must be reported under the regulation

definitions.definition: federal law amended by Dodd-Frank Act to include section 1071 requiring data collection on small business lending
definitions.relevance: establishes the legal framework and authority for small business lending data collection requirements

definitions.definition: 1975 federal law (Public Law 94-200) that has been amended multiple times and serves as a model for data collection approaches
definitions.relevance: provides precedent for the breadth and scope of financial institution data collection and reporting

definitions.definition: Public Law 111-203 that amended ECOA by adding section 1071 and directing the Bureau to adopt regulations for small business lending data collection
definitions.relevance: establishes the statutory authority and mandate for the regulation
